Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Explain default-deny, least privilege, separation of duties, and project boundary segregation; give a workplace example of each

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Use corporate SSO (Google Workspace) for all supported services; explain why native passwords must be disabled or tightly controlled; use MFA per Identification Policy for non-SSO services

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Explain what role-based access control is and how it maps permissions to job duties; know where to find the RBAC Matrix

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Identify your project boundaries; explain why cross-boundary access requires explicit approval; describe CUI boundary rules

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Determine whether a given account/role qualifies as privileged using the six-question test; use separate admin and day-to-day accounts; explain JIT elevation and logging requirements

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Create and manage service/bot accounts that are unique, least-privileged, not used for interactive login; prefer short-lived tokens; rotate static secrets quarterly

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Use strong authentication for all remote access; apply session timeouts; prefer ZTNA/identity-aware proxies over VPN

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Use native cloud IAM with least-privilege roles; avoid overly permissive grants; monitor for excessive permissions

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Explain that managed endpoints are required for Internal/Confidential/Restricted data; know that unmanaged device access is exception-only and time-boxed

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Explain that logical access depends on physical protection of devices (laptops, hardware keys); secure laptops when unattended, keep hardware keys on a keychain or secure pouch, and do not leave devices accessible to unauthorized individuals

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Submit a properly formatted access request (person, system, justification, duration, boundary, role) via ticket or Rippling workflow

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Apply the correct approval authority based on data sensitivity (IT for Public/Internal; Data/System Owner for Confidential/Restricted; CTO for privileged); ensure approver ≠ implementer

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Implement access changes in IdP/target system, record evidence in the ticket, use time-bound grants where supported

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Trigger an immediate rights review when a team member's role or project changes; right-size permissions

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Disable access immediately on separation; remove within 24 hours; revoke tokens, keys, sessions; collect evidence in offboarding ticket

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Conduct quarterly reviews for high-criticality systems and annual reviews for others; certify, remediate excess access, and retain evidence

Training Name: Training 2 — CUI Awareness & Handling
Audience: All Staff working with CUI; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Maintain strict separation between client environments and corporate systems; keep CUI inside authorized boundaries; require FedRAMP Moderate equivalence for CSPs handling CUI

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Learning Outcome: Use named sharing (no "anyone with link") for non-Public data; store secrets in approved managers; never commit secrets to code or tickets

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Understand that authentication, authorization, and privileged actions are logged and subject to audit; know the key metrics (time-to-revoke, review completion, SSO coverage)

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Describe when break-glass accounts may be used; explain the post-use review requirement (within one business day)

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Ensure supplier access is least-privileged and contractually bound; apply DFARS flow-down for CUI

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Identify the critical systems requiring SSO via Google Authentication (Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, Atlassian, Rippling, Unanet, GitLab, production cloud/CI-CD); know that non-SSO systems require ITSM tickets and explicit system approvers

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Understand that changes to access control mechanisms, roles, or RBAC mappings are subject to change enablement and must be documented; access controls must align with secure configuration baselines

Training Name: Training 1 — Security Awareness Essentials
Module: F - Governance, risk, and compliance awareness
Learning Outcome: Understand that violations can result in access revocation, disciplinary action, or contract remedies
